title: Family Law Expert Witness | Psychologists & Psychiatrists
description: Expert psychological and psychiatric assessments for family courts. Parenting capacity, attachment, domestic abuse. CVs in 24hrs. Legal Aid rates.

blocks.headline: Resolve Family Cases Faster with Court-Ready Expert Reports
blocks.subtitle: Access experienced psychologists and psychiatrists for family law proceedings. CVs within 24 hours, reports in 4–6 weeks, Legal Aid rates guaranteed. We handle everything from matching to delivery.
blocks.secondaryCtaText: Not Sure Which Expert You Need?

tabs.label: Psychology Services
tabs.content:
Our psychologists provide objective, evidence-based assessments covering the full range of family law needs. Every psychologist on our register is HCPC-registered, enhanced DBS-checked, and experienced in providing reports that meet the standards required by family courts.

Psychology assessments for family law include:

- Parenting capacity assessments
- Parent-child attachment evaluations
- Whole family assessments
- Child and adolescent psychological assessments
- Cognitive functioning and learning difficulties
- Domestic abuse impact assessments
- Sexual abuse and risk evaluations
- Ability to protect assessments
- ABE interview analysis
- Substance abuse assessments
- Family dynamics and relationship assessments
- Child development and educational needs

tabs.label: Psychiatry Services
tabs.content:
Our psychiatrists are GMC-registered, Section 12 approved medical professionals. They provide expert psychiatric opinion, diagnosis, and treatment recommendations based on ICD-10 and DSM-5 criteria. Where a case requires medical-level assessment of mental health, our psychiatrists deliver thorough, court-ready reports.

Psychiatric assessments for family law include:

- Mental health diagnosis and assessment
- Personality disorder evaluations
- Effects of trauma and PTSD
- Capacity assessments (mental capacity to parent)
- Substance misuse and dependency
- Care and contact proceedings
- Risk assessments (violence neglect self-harm)
- Review of medical notes and psychiatric histories
- Treatment recommendations and prognosis

tabs.label: When Do You Need Both?
tabs.content:
Many family law cases benefit from both psychological and psychiatric input. A psychologist assesses behaviour, cognition, and family dynamics. A psychiatrist provides medical diagnosis and treatment recommendations. Your dedicated Client Manager can advise on which expert type — or combination — best fits your case.

Not sure? Use our free tool to find out: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? → /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

blocks.imageAlt: Supportive hands representing sensitive family case handling
blocks.heading: We Understand the Sensitivity
blocks.bodyText: Family law cases involve vulnerable children and parents in crisis. Our experts are experienced in working with families going through the most difficult times. We handle each case with empathy, professionalism, and the urgency your court deadlines demand.

steps.title: Tell Us About Your Case
steps.description: Call us on 01306 879 975 or complete our online instruction form. A dedicated Client Manager will take a detailed brief of your requirements.

steps.title: Receive Expert CVs
steps.description: Within 24 hours, we'll send you 2–3 CVs and quotes from psychologists or psychiatrists matched to your case's specialism, location, and timeline.

steps.title: We Handle Everything
steps.description: Once you instruct, we manage all assessment bookings — including complex multi-party logistics — liaison with all solicitors, and administration.

steps.title: Court-Ready Report Delivered
steps.description: Your report is 100% quality-checked for accuracy, completeness, and compliance before delivery. Standard turnaround is 4–6 weeks; fast-track available.

blocks.heading: Not Sure What Expert You Need?
blocks.bodyText: Family law cases often require specialist knowledge — but choosing between a psychologist and a psychiatrist isn't always straightforward. Our free tool helps you work out which expert your case needs in under 2 minutes.

blocks.heading: Common Family Law Case Types We Support
blocks.subheading: Our experts have experience across the full range of public and private family law proceedings.

cards.title: Public Law Care Proceedings
cards.description: We provide expert assessments in care proceedings, helping courts understand parenting capacity, risk, and the needs of children in care.

cards.title: Private Law Contact & Residence
cards.description: Assessments for disputes over child arrangements, including custody, contact, and relocation cases.

cards.title: Parenting Capacity Assessments
cards.description: Detailed evaluations of a parent's ability to meet their child's physical, emotional, and developmental needs.

cards.title: Domestic Abuse & Violence
cards.description: Psychological and psychiatric impact assessments for cases involving domestic violence, coercive control, and abuse.

cards.title: Substance Misuse & Dependency
cards.description: Expert assessment of substance misuse patterns, their impact on parenting, and treatment prognosis.

cards.title: Capacity & Mental Health
cards.description: Psychiatric evaluations of mental health conditions affecting parenting capacity, including personality disorders and PTSD.

rows.col1: We book and manage all assessments
HCPC/GMC registered, DBS checked, report sample reviewed
Same or less than instructing directly

blocks.heading: How We've Helped in Family Law Cases

caseStudies.title: Complex Logistics in a Family Law Case
caseStudies.summary: A specialist family law firm needed assessments of a large dysfunctional family across multiple locations, with domestic violence and funding uncertainty. Our Client Manager coordinated assessments of eight individuals, managed the timetable, and delivered a quality-checked report on time. The children returned home on a supervision order.

caseStudies.title: Court Helped Decide in Care Proceedings
caseStudies.summary: A court needed a psychiatric assessment to make an informed decision on a mother's ability to parent. We matched a Section 12 approved psychiatrist who delivered a thorough report within the court's timeline.

caseStudies.title: Assessing Parental Access in Care Proceedings
caseStudies.summary: In sensitive care proceedings involving decisions about parental access, we provided an experienced psychiatrist to assess the situation and deliver a clear, evidence-based report to support the court's decision.

faqs.question: How quickly can I receive CVs for a family law case?
faqs.answer: We typically provide 2–3 CVs and full quotes within 24 hours of receiving your brief. For urgent cases, we can often respond even faster. Each CV includes the expert's qualifications, relevant experience, and fee breakdown as required by the Legal Aid Agency.

faqs.question: Do your experts work at Legal Aid rates?
faqs.answer: Yes, all our psychologists and psychiatrists work at Legal Aid rates for publicly funded family law cases. We provide full fee breakdowns with every quote so there are no surprises. For privately funded cases, we provide transparent quotes upfront.

faqs.question: Can you work within the 26-week pre-proceedings timetable?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We understand the statutory timelines in family proceedings and regularly deliver reports within these frameworks. Standard turnaround is 4–6 weeks, and we offer a fast-track service for cases with tighter deadlines.

faqs.question: Do I need a psychologist or a psychiatrist for my family law case?
faqs.answer: It depends on what the court needs to understand. A psychologist assesses behaviour, parenting capacity, attachment, and family dynamics. A psychiatrist provides medical diagnosis of mental health conditions and treatment recommendations. Many cases benefit from both. Use our free tool to check: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? → /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

faqs.question: How do you ensure the quality of expert reports?
faqs.answer: Every report is 100% proofread and quality-checked by our Client Management team before it reaches you. We verify that all instructions have been addressed, that recommendations are clear and actionable, and that the report meets family court standards. We also vet all experts before they join our register, reviewing sample reports, verifying HCPC/GMC registration, and checking enhanced DBS status.

faqs.question: Can you handle multi-party cases with complex logistics?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is one of our strengths. Our Client Managers are experienced in coordinating assessments across multiple parties, locations, and solicitors. We handle all booking, liaison, and administration so you can focus on your case.

faqs.question: Can I speak to the expert directly?
faqs.answer: Your dedicated Client Manager is your primary point of contact and is fully briefed on your case. They handle all queries, administration, and coordination. In rare circumstances where direct contact with the expert is appropriate, your Client Manager can arrange this.

blocks.heading: Need a Family Law Expert Witness?
blocks.bodyText: Whether it's a parenting capacity assessment, care proceedings, or a complex multi-party case, we'll match you with the right expert. We handle everything from instruction to delivery.
